message:
feat(core): add RCH-aware command classification to build coordinator and policy engine

Extend the build coordination module with functions to detect when heavy
cargo commands (build, check, test, bench, clippy) are running without
being routed through rch (Remote Compilation Helper). The policy engine
now enforces this by requiring approval when a robot actor tries to run
a heavy cargo command without the rch exec wrapper, preventing local
compile storms on the host machine.

build_coord.rs:
- Add command_uses_rch(): detect rch exec -- prefix in commands
- Add is_heavy_cargo_command(): classify build/check/test/bench/clippy
- Add requires_rch_offload(): combines both checks
- Add recommended_rch_prefix(): suggest the correct rch invocation
- Refactor detect_cargo_command() to use tokenizer for robustness
  with env vars, sudo, shell wrappers, and rch-wrapped commands
- Add HEAVY_CARGO_SUBCOMMANDS constant
- Add comprehensive tests for all new functions

policy.rs:
- Add RCH_HEAVY_COMPUTE_RULE_ID policy rule
- Integrate requires_rch_offload check in SendText evaluation for
  robot actors (human actors are unaffected)
- Return require_approval decision with evidence (rch_required,
  rch_recommended_prefix) so the agent can self-correct
- Add tests: robot blocked, human allowed, rch-wrapped allowed

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
